source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two identical antennas are placed in the \( \theta = \pi/2 \) plane as shown in the figure. The elements have equal amplitude excitation with a 180° polarity difference, operating at wavelength \( \lambda \). The correct value of the magnitude of the far-zone resultant electric field strength normalized with that of a single element, both computed for \( \phi = 0 \), is
